body:
status=blocked
reason=Bash tool sandbox is unusable in this execution worktree — every shell invocation fails immediately with: ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-24949caf-20260708T152102-77b1e4a1' This is the harness's per-session sandbox directory under /tmp/claude-501, not this repo's disk. It fails even for trivial no-op commands (`true`, `echo ok`), confirming the host's /tmp (or the claude-501 sandbox mount) is out of space/inodes rather than anything the bead's code change could affect. Retried the shell 3 times; ENOSPC persisted every time. Effect: I could not run `go test`, `git status/add/commit`, or `lefthook run pre-commit` — every AC in this bead (ddx-24949caf) requires at least one of those. I read the relevant code (cli/internal/server/server.go Shutdown(), cli/internal/server/workers.go waitForStoppedRecord/Shutdown, cli/cmd/server_service.go) and confirmed the root cause described in the bead (unbounded <-s.supervisorDone wait at server.go:379-386, ahead of the already-bounded worker shutdown wait at workers.go:1696-1703) still matches current HEAD, but did not make code edits because without a shell there is no way to compile, test, or commit them, and an uncommitted/unverified change would violate "do not commit red code" / "commit exactly once when green". This is a host resource-exhaustion problem external to the ddx-24949caf bead scope (server shutdown ordering). It needs operator or orchestrator action to free /tmp/claude-501 space (or reschedule this execution onto a worktree/host with available space) before this bead can be retried. verification_retry_hint: rerun `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` once shell access is restored, before assuming this rationale is stale.
suggested_action=recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared

no_changes
rationale: status: blocked
reason: Bash tool sandbox is unusable in this execution worktree — every shell invocation fails immediately with:
  ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-24949caf-20260708T152102-77b1e4a1'
This is the harness's per-session sandbox directory under /tmp/claude-501, not this repo's disk. It fails even for trivial no-op commands (`true`, `echo ok`), confirming the host's /tmp (or the claude-501 sandbox mount) is out of space/inodes rather than anything the bead's code change could affect. Retried the shell 3 times; ENOSPC persisted every time.

Effect: I could not run `go test`, `git status/add/commit`, or `lefthook run pre-commit` — every AC in this bead (ddx-24949caf) requires at least one of those. I read the relevant code (cli/internal/server/server.go Shutdown(), cli/internal/server/workers.go waitForStoppedRecord/Shutdown, cli/cmd/server_service.go) and confirmed the root cause described in the bead (unbounded <-s.supervisorDone wait at server.go:379-386, ahead of the already-bounded worker shutdown wait at workers.go:1696-1703) still matches current HEAD, but did not make code edits because without a shell there is no way to compile, test, or commit them, and an uncommitted/unverified change would violate "do not commit red code" / "commit exactly once when green".

This is a host resource-exhaustion problem external to the ddx-24949caf bead scope (server shutdown ordering). It needs operator or orchestrator action to free /tmp/claude-501 space (or reschedule this execution onto a worktree/host with available space) before this bead can be retried.

verification_retry_hint: rerun `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` once shell access is restored, before assuming this rationale is stale.

{"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"Add `TestServerShutdownBoundsSupervisorWaitBeforeWorkerShutdown` (or an equivalently named test) in `cli/internal/server/server_shutdown_test.go` that simulates a supervisor goroutine stuck after cancellation and proves `Shutdown()` still invokes worker cleanup within a bounded duration.","coverage":"covered by child 1: server: bound shutdown supervisor wait before worker cleanup: part 1"},{"parent_ac":"Add or update a worker-manager shutdown regression so a live managed worker with descendant git/provider subprocesses is reaped through the existing worker shutdown path even when supervisor cancellation is slow.","coverage":"covered by child 2: server: bound shutdown supervisor wait before worker cleanup: part 2"},{"parent_ac":"`Server.Shutdown()` must not block indefinitely on `supervisorDone`; timeout behavior must return/log a useful error while continuing to close bead hub and stop land coordinators.","coverage":"covered by child 2: server: bound shutdown supervisor wait before worker cleanup: part 2"},{"parent_ac":"`cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/...` exits 0.","coverage":"covered by child 3: server: bound shutdown supervisor wait before worker cleanup: part 3"},{"parent_ac":"`lefthook run pre-commit` exits 0.","coverage":"covered by child 3: server: bound shutdown supervisor wait before worker cleanup: part 3"}],"child_ids":["ddx-b7d2cd83","ddx-d0fce58c","ddx-5c8eb35a"],"rationale":"deterministic fallback split after agent decomposer returned invalid output"}

summary: closed as dead-intermediate bead (all children closed, execution-eligible=false)
